title: COUNT(DISTINCT ?var) for unbounded ?var projects incorrect value
description:
Virtuoso (tested on version 07.00.3203 deployed on DBPedia) projects incorrect value for COUNT(DISTINCT ?var) when the ?var isn't bounded. Section 18.5.1.2 of the SPARQL 1.1 Query specification states that in such cases zero should be returned. However, Virtuoso returns 1.
For example, the following query (see results) shows this incorrect behaviour:
PREFIX dc:   <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/>
PREFIX foaf: <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/>

SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT ?unbound) AS ?count)
WHERE {
  ?s foaf:name [] .
  OPTIONAL {
    ?s dc:nonexistent ?unbound .
  }
}
GROUP BY ?s
LIMIT 1

More detailed discussion of this issue can be found in this question on answers.semanticweb.com.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value INT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Alice', NULL), (2, 'Bob', 10), (3, 'Charlie', 20);
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT value) AS count FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT name) AS count FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'A%';
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN value IS NOT NULL THEN value ELSE -1 END) AS count FROM test_table;
key_bug_features: COUNT(DISTINCT ?var) with unbounded ?var returns incorrect value
